text.title: Freelance Tax Cashflow Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding Freelance Tax Cashflow Basics. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Self-employment + income tax estimated payments MUST be made quarterly. Missed quarter = penalty + tracked as a kill condition.
rule.rationale: US IRS + many local tax authorities require quarterly payments; missing them compounds penalties.

rule.statement: For cross-border B2B EU invoices the invoice MUST carry the reverse-charge notation + buyer VAT ID. Domestic + non-EU invoices use standard VAT/sales tax.
rule.rationale: Reverse-charge missing on EU B2B = potential VAT liability on freelancer side.

rule.statement: Entity check (sole-trader → LLC / Ltd / equivalent) MUST be re-run when annual revenue crosses $50k OR when liability exposure (1 vs many clients) changes.

rationale.source: IRS + UK HMRC sole-trader vs LLC guidance

rule.rationale: Below ~$50k, sole-trader simplicity dominates; above, liability + tax structure benefit from LLC.

rule.statement: Business revenue + expenses MUST flow through a separate business bank account (not personal). Mixed accounts forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mixed accounts kill audit defensibility + entity protection.

rule.statement: Before any rate-jump, niche-pivot, or capacity expansion, personal runway (savings ÷ monthly burn) MUST be ≥6 months. Below 6m, defer the change.
rule.rationale: Without runway, rate / niche changes become forced concessions to keep cashflow.
